visual_features.overall_style: Clean, modern magazine-style layout with a family-friendly, slightly playful brand (through color) sitting on a mostly minimal, white card-based interface. It feels editorial rather than app-like: strong hero image, right-column schedule, centered logo and location.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: 1) Brand logo at top center in bright colors; 2) Location text 'LINCOLN, NE' in vivid pink; 3) Primary horizontal navigation; 4) Large hero image carousel in a white card; 5) Right-hand 'TODAY'S EVENTS' pink-topped sidebar card; 6) Individual event titles in pink, with times in light gray. Hierarchy is created via size (hero image dominates), color contrast (hot pink and black on white), and positional prominence (top-center logo, right sidebar panel).
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Generous outer margins around the main white content card against a light gray page background. Within cards, padding is moderate (roughly 24–32px) giving images and text breathing room. The nav bar has ample horizontal spacing between items, and vertical spacing between logo, location, navigation, and content is consistent and airy.
unique_elements: Hand-drawn/scribbled underline under the 'Save on Local Family Fun' navigation item, adding a casual, playful accent.
Bright pink vertical 'TODAY'S EVENTS' card acting as a prominent right sidebar schedule widget.
Large hero carousel composed of a collage/grid of photos with a semi-transparent overlay panel for headline text.
Centered city/location label in uppercase pink between logo and main navigation.

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Appears to follow a 12-column grid with a centered max-width container. The hero content uses an approximate 8-column main area (carousel) and 4-column right sidebar (Today’s Events).
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Centered, fixed-width layout with a global header on top, followed by a two-column content band: main hero carousel on the left and a vertical event list sidebar on the right, all inside a prominent white content card on a light gray background.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top-to-bottom: (1) Global page background; (2) Centered logo strip; (3) Location strip; (4) Horizontal navigation bar; (5) Main content card containing a left hero slider and right 'Today’s Events' sidebar. Subsequent sections (not visible) likely stack vertically beneath this primary card.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Overall center alignment for branding (logo, location). Navigation items are horizontally aligned and evenly spaced. Main content is left-aligned within a centered container. The 'TODAY'S EVENTS' title is left-aligned within the pink header bar; event times and titles are left-aligned, creating a clear vertical reading line.
responsive_hints: Two-column hero + sidebar suggests a collapse to single-column layout on smaller screens (sidebar stacking under the hero carousel).
Centered logo and nav imply that on mobile, navigation may collapse into a menu icon while preserving centered branding.
Cards with consistent padding and simple grid-based structure are well-suited to stacking behavior on narrow viewports.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow: logo block → location label → single-line horizontal nav → large white content card. Inside the card, there is a split layout: on the left, a hero carousel with a grid of event photos and a centered semi-transparent headline overlay; on the right, a vertical sidebar card titled 'TODAY'S EVENTS' with a pink header bar and a stacked list of time-stamped events. Navigation text and sidebar use simple textual links; the carousel has left/right arrow controls at horizontal midline.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: The header/logo/nav area uses roughly the top 20–25% of the visible viewport with generous vertical spacing, but minimal heavy content. The main white content card takes up the majority of the visible viewport. Within that card, about 65–70% of the horizontal width is dedicated to the hero image carousel and 30–35% to the 'TODAY'S EVENTS' sidebar. Outer page margins and the area above/below the card are filled with light gray background, creating a framed effect around the main content.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Moderately spacious. The hero area is visually dense due to multiple photos, but this is balanced by large margins and a clear overlay text block. The event sidebar list is compact but readable, with small vertical spacing between items. Overall, the design prioritizes clarity over packing maximum items on screen; there is enough whitespace that content doesn’t feel crowded.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: Primary flow is a strong vertical path: eye hits the colorful 'Macaroni KID' logo at the top, drops to the location label, then to the navigation. From there, the large hero carousel image pulls the eye down and slightly left. The bold vertical pink 'TODAY'S EVENTS' panel on the right then attracts attention, creating a secondary rightward pull. The user is likely to scan within the hero headline, then move rightward to skim today’s event list from top to bottom.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Explicit filled buttons aren’t strongly featured in the visible area; instead, subtle control-like elements appear: (1) Carousel navigation arrows: small circular or rounded-square black buttons at the far left and right edges of the carousel, vertically centered, with white arrow icons. They likely have no border, solid fill (#000000) with white icon, 100% round or 16–20px radius, about 32–40px in diameter, and perhaps a faint drop shadow. (2) Text links such as 'SEE ALL »' in the sidebar: styled as inline text CTAs in pink with a right-pointing angle glyph, no background or border, relying on color and positioning as a call to action.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Carousel arrows: <button> or <div role='button'> elements positioned absolutely inside the carousel container, likely with SVG or icon font for arrows. Text CTA buttons: <a> elements styled as buttons/links, possibly with a .link-cta or .see-all class to differentiate from standard links.

ui_patterns.visual_description: The main content appears as a large white card floating on a light gray background. Corners are slightly rounded (about 4–8px radius). The card likely has a subtle drop shadow (soft, low spread, neutral gray) or at least a clear separation via background contrast. Inside, content (carousel and sidebar) respects a uniform inner padding (~24–32px). The 'TODAY'S EVENTS' sidebar itself is a card-within-card: white interior with a bold pink header bar across the top, full width, with white uppercase title text and no visible border; the rest of the card body is simple white with text-only event rows.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Outer card: <section> or <div class='card main-card'> wrapping the carousel and sidebar, implemented with a flex or grid layout for the two columns. Sidebar card: <aside class='card events-today'> featuring a header element (<header> or <div class='card-header'>) with the pink bar, followed by <div class='card-body'> containing a list of events (<ul> or <div> list) and a footer link (e.g., 'SEE ALL »').

ui_patterns.visual_description: No explicit form fields are visible in the screenshot. However, there is a search icon in the header, implying a search input toggle. Given the aesthetic, expected form fields would be full-width text inputs with subtle 1px light gray borders (#E0E0E0), 4px border radius, white background, and inner padding of 8–12px. Labels likely appear above fields in a small, medium-gray text. Focus state may use a pink or teal border highlight to align with brand colors.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Forms will likely use stacked label-input pairs: <form> with <label for='id'> above each <input>. Groupings may be wrapped in <div class='form-group'> with consistent bottom margins. Search may be a single <form> with a text <input> and a submit <button> icon, or a hidden input revealed when clicking the search icon.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Primary navigation is a horizontal text menu under the location label. Items (Subscribe, Articles, Guides, Events, Directory, Become a Publisher, Save on Local Family Fun, Explore Communities) are set in a clean sans-serif, upper/lowercase, with ample horizontal spacing (approx 24–32px between items). Text is dark gray/black on white. Active or emphasized items use color or additional treatment: the 'Save on Local Family Fun' link has a playful scribbled underline, and external-link icon appears on it. Hover/active states are likely underlines or color changes to pink. The search icon on the far right of the nav line acts as a minimal icon-only navigation control.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Standard <nav> with an unordered list <ul> of <li><a> items, arranged in a flex row with space-between or gap spacing. Special nav items (external link, scribble underline) have additional classes. The search icon is likely an <button> or <a> with an SVG icon, possibly positioned at the end of the flex row. Location ('LINCOLN, NE') is a separate centered text row between logo and nav, likely a <div class='location'> or <h2> styled as a tag/label.

ui_patterns.visual_description: The 'TODAY'S EVENTS' sidebar uses a vertical list of events with a light typographic hierarchy: small gray time stamp on the left/top, and event title in pink to the right or directly below the time. Some entries feature a small heart icon in pink preceding the title. Items are spaced with about 8–12px vertical margin; there are no explicit borders or lines between them, relying on whitespace and alignment for separation. A 'SEE ALL »' text link at the bottom aligns right or bottom-right, in pink, indicating extended list access.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Likely implemented as <ul class='event-list'> with each event in <li>. Inside each <li>, a small text block for time (<span class='event-time'>) and another for the title (<a class='event-title'> or <span>), possibly arranged with flexbox (time and title side by side) or stacked. Icons/hearts are inline SVGs or icon-font <i> elements placed before the title. The 'SEE ALL »' is an <a> outside the list or as a final list item styled differently.

heading.visual_style: The non-logo headings (e.g., 'TODAY'S EVENTS') appear as bold, all-caps sans serif with clear, straight lines and moderate width—readable but slightly assertive, fitting a magazine-like, modern aesthetic.

body.visual_style: Body and navigation text appear as a clean, neutral sans serif with good x-height and legibility, likely a humanist or grotesque style designed for comfortable reading across articles and event descriptions.

spacing_system.component_padding: Core components such as cards and sidebar panels use approximately 24px horizontal and vertical padding (3 × 8px) inside their borders. Smaller elements like list items and nav links use 8–12px vertical padding and 16–24px horizontal padding.
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major sections (e.g., between logo, location, navigation, and main content card) is roughly 24–32px, and the main content card is separated from the page edges with 40–64px of outer margin on desktop.

interaction_hints: Navigation links likely use hover states with color change to the primary pink (#EC008C) and/or underline appearance; the scribble underline may darken or shift slightly on hover for 'Save on Local Family Fun'.
Carousel arrows are expected to show hover states with slight brightness change, shadow intensification, or scale-up (e.g., 1.05) and an accessible focus outline.
Event titles probably change color or underline on hover, emphasizing clickability, while times remain neutral gray.
Smooth transitions (150–250ms) for color, background, and transform changes would match the polished, modern aesthetic without feeling overly animated.

Micro-interactions could include a subtle slide or fade transition between carousel slides and a gentle hover lift (1–2px translateY with soft shadow) on cards when they are used elsewhere in the site.
